class.name: AudioStreamInteractive
class.brief_description: Stores multiple sub-[AudioStream]s, which can be set to play interactively, with different settings for transitions.
class.description: [AudioStreamInteractive] can hold multiple streams which in this resource are called [b]clips[/b]. Each clip can transition to another automatically with [method set_clip_auto_advance] and [method set_clip_auto_advance_next_clip], or manually with [method AudioStreamPlaybackInteractive.switch_to_clip] (an existing playback can be obtained with [method AudioStreamPlayer.get_stream_playback], [method AudioStreamPlayer2D.get_stream_playback], and [method AudioStreamPlayer3D.get_stream_playback], after [AudioStreamInteractive] starts playing). Transitions can be edited via the Transition Editor, which can be opened in the inspector, by opening the stream's properties from an audio stream player and clicking [b]Edit Transitions[/b], or via code, using [method add_transition]. This stream is ideal to play music tracks and transition between each other depending on context, for example, switching from calm ambience to a chase sequence music track, or switching music tracks representing phase one and phase two of a combat sequence. [b]Note:[/b] Because [AudioStreamWAV] does not have any tempo and beat information, beat-sync features in [AudioStreamInteractive] are not used for that stream resource like it is for others. Consider using [AudioStreamOggVorbis] for beat-synced transitions.

method.name: add_transition
method.description: Adds a transition between two or more clips. Provide the indices of the source and destination clips ([param from_clip] and [param to_clip] respectively), or use the [constant CLIP_ANY] constant to indicate that the transition happens from any clip [param to_clip] or [param from_clip] to any clip. * [param from_time] indicates the moment in [param from_clip] that the transition will begin after a switch is triggered. See [enum TransitionFromTime]. * [param to_time] indicates the playback position in [param to_clip] that the playback will start from. See [enum TransitionToTime]. * [param fade_mode] indicates how the fade will happen between clips. If unsure, just use [constant FADE_AUTOMATIC] which uses the most common type of fade depending on [param to_time]. See [enum FadeMode]. * [param fade_beats] indicates how many beats the fade will take. Using decimals is allowed. The transition will be beat-synced if [param from_clip] is imported with accurate tempo (see [ResourceImporterMP3] and [ResourceImporterOggVorbis]). Otherwise, this parameter works as transition speed. * [param use_filler_clip] indicates that there will be a filler clip used between [param from_clip] and [param to_clip]. * [param filler_clip] indicates the index of the filler clip. * [param hold_previous] determines if [param from_clip] will be remembered. This can be used together with [constant AUTO_ADVANCE_RETURN_TO_HOLD] to return to [param from_clip] after another clip is done playing.

method.name: erase_transition
method.description: Erases a transition by providing [param from_clip] and [param to_clip] clip indices. [constant CLIP_ANY] can be used for either argument or both.

method.name: get_clip_auto_advance
method.description: Returns which auto-advance mode [param clip_index] has. See [method set_clip_auto_advance] and [enum AutoAdvanceMode].

method.name: get_clip_auto_advance_next_clip
method.description: Returns the clip index which the clip of the given [param clip_index] will auto-advance to.

method.name: get_clip_name
method.description: Returns the name of the clip of the given [param clip_index].

method.name: get_clip_stream
method.description: Returns the [AudioStream] of the given [param clip_index].

method.name: get_transition_fade_beats
method.description: Returns how many beats a transition will take between clips, if the source clip has tempo and beat information. See [method add_transition].

method.name: get_transition_fade_mode
method.description: Returns the fade type for a transition. See [method add_transition] and [enum FadeMode].

method.name: get_transition_filler_clip
method.description: Returns the index of the filler clip used for a transition. Returns [code]0[/code] if no filler clip is used, and [code]-1[/code] along with an error if the specified transition does not exist. See [method add_transition]. [b]Note:[/b] This method is best used with [method has_transition] and [method is_transition_using_filler_clip].

method.name: get_transition_from_time
method.description: Returns when a transition will happen after [method AudioStreamPlaybackInteractive.switch_to_clip] is called. See [method add_transition] and [enum TransitionFromTime].

method.name: get_transition_list
method.description: Returns the list of transitions. Each pair represents a source and a destination clip. Every even index (starting at [code]0[/code]) represents the index of a source clip, while every odd index represents the index of a destination clip. Filler clips are not included.

method.name: get_transition_to_time
method.description: Returns the playback position that the destination clip starts at, after a transition. See [method add_transition] and [enum TransitionToTime].

method.name: has_transition
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the given transition exists.

method.name: is_transition_holding_previous
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the source clip in a transition uses the [i]hold previous[/i] functionality. See [method add_transition].

method.name: is_transition_using_filler_clip
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if a transition uses the [i]filler clip[/i] functionality. See [method add_transition].

method.name: set_clip_auto_advance
method.description: Sets the auto-advance mode of the given [param clip_index]. See [enum AutoAdvanceMode].

method.name: set_clip_auto_advance_next_clip
method.description: Sets the index of the clip which [param clip_index] will auto-advance to when finished. If [param clip_index] has looping enabled, then auto-advance will be ignored.

method.name: set_clip_name
method.description: Sets the name of [param clip_index], for easier identification.

method.name: set_clip_stream
method.description: Sets the [param stream] of the given [param clip_index].

members.member: Number of clips contained in this audio stream.
Index of the initial clip, which will be played first when this stream is played.

constants.constant: Transitions as soon as possible, does not wait for any specific playback position.

Transitions when the playback position of the source clip reaches the next beat. This only works accurately if the clip is imported with the correct tempo set. See also [ResourceImporterOggVorbis] or [ResourceImporterMP3]. Falls back to [constant TRANSITION_FROM_TIME_IMMEDIATE] if the clip is imported without an assigned tempo.

Transitions when the playback position of the source clip reaches the next bar. This only works accurately if the clip is imported with the correct tempo [i]and[/i] beats per bar information set. See also [ResourceImporterOggVorbis] or [ResourceImporterMP3]. Falls back to [constant TRANSITION_FROM_TIME_IMMEDIATE] if the clip is imported without an assigned tempo or beats per bar information.
Transitions when the clip finishes playing. This only works if the clip has a length greater than [code]0.0[/code]. Falls back to [constant TRANSITION_FROM_TIME_IMMEDIATE] otherwise.

Transitions to the same playback position in the destination clip. This is useful when both clips have exactly the same length and the music should fade between them. [b]Note:[/b] This only works if the clip transitions before it finishes. And if the playback position is past the end of the destination clip, the destination clip will play from the start.
Transitions to the start of the destination clip.
Transitions to the last played playback position in the destination clip, if there was a previous transition from that clip. Otherwise, plays from the start of the destination clip.
Does not use fade for the transition. This is preferred when transitioning from the end of one clip to the beginning of another, and each clip already has a fade.
Uses a fade-in in the destination clip, after the source clip finishes without a fade-out at the transition time.
Uses a fade-out in the source clip, while the destination clip starts without a fade-in at the transition time.
Uses a cross-fade between clips. The source clip fades out while the destination clip fades in.
Uses [constant FADE_OUT] if [method get_transition_to_time] returns [constant TRANSITION_TO_TIME_START]. Uses [constant FADE_CROSS] otherwise. It is recommended to use this by default.
Disables auto-advance. The clip will not automatically switch to another after it finishes. This is the default behavior.
Enables auto-advance. The clip will automatically switch to another after it finishes. The clip it will switch to must be specified.

Enables auto-advance, but instead of switching to a specified clip after it finishes, this audio stream will return to the last clip played with [code]hold_previous[/code] enabled. See [method add_transition]. [b]Note:[/b] This auto-advance mode only works if the previous clip is not the one with [code]hold_previous[/code] enabled. Otherwise the clip finishes without switching.
This constant describes that any clip is valid for a specific transition as either source or destination.
